title: Honeycomb toffee
subtitle: Crisp, airy toffee sponge made with bicarbonate of soda
excerpt: Honeycomb toffee made by cooking sugar and golden syrup to 150°C, then whisking in bicarbonate of soda to produce the characteristic aerated, sponge-like texture. Sets in under 30 minutes. Makes one slab.
sourceNotes: Honeycomb toffee (also called cinder toffee, sponge toffee, or hokey pokey) is documented in British domestic confectionery from the nineteenth century onward. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: a cinder toffee made from sugar and golden syrup cooked to crack stage with bicarbonate of soda added at the end is documented in the confectionery chapter. The USDA public-domain publications on sugar chemistry document the foaming reaction between bicarbonate of soda and the hot acidic sugar syrup (from the golden syrup) that produces the aerated structure.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keep in a sealed tin with a sachet of silica gel; moisture softens the honeycomb within a few hours. Best eaten the same day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: hard-crack stage, 150°C; the syrup is pale amber before the bicarbonate goes in

content.text: The honeycomb structure forms in seconds when the bicarbonate of soda hits the hot sugar: the alkali reacts with the acid in the golden syrup and produces carbon dioxide, which foams through the toffee before it sets. Have the prepared tin and the bicarbonate already measured before the sugar is cooked; there is no time to search once it reaches temperature. If hot syrup touches skin, run cold water over it for at least ten minutes and seek medical care if needed.

items.prepNote: about 1½ tsp; sifted and ready to use

content.text: Line a 20 × 20 cm baking tray with lightly oiled baking paper. Put the {{caster-sugar}} and {{golden-syrup}} in a medium, deep saucepan. Stir over low heat until the sugar dissolves. Stop stirring, clip in the sugar thermometer, and raise the heat to medium. Cook without stirring until the syrup reaches 150°C; 9–12 minutes. The colour should be pale amber. Remove from the heat and immediately tip in the measured {{bicarbonate-of-soda}} — the mixture will foam violently and double in size. Whisk quickly for 5 seconds only, then pour into the prepared tray without spreading. Leave to cool and set completely; about 30 minutes. Break into pieces to serve.
